reviews.review_text: if you're in asia every server is practically unplayable due to the high ping other than the checkpoint pve gamemodes

its great FPS, vs AI (PVE) still fun.
Dont forget turn on ur cross play in matchmaking setting, and optimize the region of ur matchmaking to get better ping.

over 100 hours in the game never cheated, but suddenly i keep getting kicked out due to anti cheat violations?? and it keeps popping up the last few weeks. searched online and there doesn't seem to be a fix for it. I absolutely love this game but this is just ridiculous.
modern call of duty but actually good instead of nicki minaj and obnoxious slop eating streamers
An extremely fun shooter that you have to be tactical in or you will pay for it! Updates pretty often and has a large amount of guns to choose through to take your enemies down!
ac units don't vent anywhere. completely broke my immersion. absolutely unplayable.

Nicely done, pushed an update that makes the game think I'm cheating and crashes my game every time I load into a match, I love this game and it is such a better experience on PC but not if it's not even playable.
Good fun game that almost anyone can just jump into and just play and have fun.
severely underrated game. Intense, realistic and tactical multiplayer game that is the perfect mix of realistic / arcade gameplay.
Ruthless wave defence shooter. Its realistic in a sense, and hard to master, worth trying.

Game has the potential to be a great MIl SIm FPS, Unfortunately............. The developers of the game dont want to commit any resources or critical thinking to the game due to other projects/Funds...... Played this game for years now both on console and PC. All they really add to the game is a little bit here and a little bit there.... Its not enough to keep you coming back consistently.

Don't play people are losers and will sweat their lives away and the developers don't give a ♥♥♥♥ they release a quarter a year update, or semi year update with a new weapon for each faction and maybe a map if they care but mostly one weapon per faction and a buttload of paid cosmetics it shows where their intentions are and how little they care about the game other than the micro transaction slop. Insurgency: Sandstorm is an fantastic tactical hardcore FPS game, which brought me lots of fun for over 80 hours.
I just stopped playing, because i have no friends anymore, which could join me there. But i love the tactical Gameplay and the ingame Immersion. Also everyone in the team has to know his role, if you don't you lose.^^ You also have different game modes. My favorite one is called hardcore checkpoint. :3

To make it short: If you love hardcore tactical FPS built on lethal close-quarters combat and ruthless intensity, this game is for you. :3

The Game is objectively fun, its got a good loop, the modes are great, the PVP is fantastic and the PVE is hours of fun, I have at least 100 Hours on xbox Series X alone. 

The reason I don't reccomend this game is due to the lack of support or acknowledgement of a key issue with the Modio system, Joining a custom game means downloading hundreds of mods all at once, with no way to stop them or remove them fully. if you "delete" the mods locally on your device opening game means you will just reinstall all of these mods. and if you have 2 storage drives on your PC its a complete pain in the ass to remove said mods 

the fact its been over 5 years with no fix to this or any acknowledgement over the fact that this is an ongoing issue baffles me. 

If you wanna try it out - Get the game if its on Sale (it usually is) 

have at least 160+ gigs available if you incase you ever want to try a custom game. because you will forced to download them onto your system and they ain't leaving until you uninstall and remove from your files manually after the fact.


overall - Games fun, great loops, But the fact a full lustrum has passed and these issues persist ruins it long term for me

This game was enjoyable all around, the gun customization and the more realistic types of shotgun damage really made the game quiet nice. My only issue is, is that this game has always been near death and as of right now is nearly fully dead leaving most game modes empty, making it a full waste of  money as the ai are not at all worth fighting. Current state I'll give this game a 3/10, would have been a 7 if it had players.

This use to be our 'go to' game. Now the mods do not work at all anymore so as far as we are concerned this game is dead. Don't waste your money unless you want to settle for the stock maps and guns which get boring really quick.
my 2005 toyota camry 700,000km with 3 engine changes in the last decades plus a broken battery that has to be manually jump charged everytime i start the engine runs way better than this game
Very fun game, but the game has one foot in the grave unfortunately. (Still gets enough players to find a match within a few minutes.)

Guns, calibers and hitboxes are top-nothch...
Most realistic multiplayer game out there. You can truly feel war atmosphere
This game runs like a dream, pvp, pve, and community servers are a  lot of fun, highly recommend this game
its fun the gore amazing its not like other games where it takes about 20 bullets to  kill a player its got realistic kickback depending on the weapon you use

A great tactical game. You can play PVE and PVP in squad based matches.
Both have various modes and it's not difficult to find a match.

8.78777/10 - I keep hearing screams, gunshots and explosions even 10 minutes after playing
"Over there!" 
"ENEMIES!" 
*Cellphone ringing* 
*Explosion sound*

I have to say, after many years of supporting these devs. 
Their game, still stands out in todays TAC FPS category!
The performance is great, the maps are fun, the gunplay is like tarkov or arma, just in a Smaller map and a different MP experience! The voip to other teams is always hilarious, 
I hope they continue growing!

Was a good game, still is but it feels different. i bought it again on steam and i still enjoy it but wish they would actually work on the singleplayer co-op story rather then the skins. 

But what can you expect from a studio that was bought by embracer group?

There were some optimisation issues that seems to be fixed now so thats great.

If your looking for another fun fps than yes, get if you can or at least on sale. Play offline levels against bots which is what I do or go online & challenge yourself against other players. Be advised this game has a steep learning curve. Pick out your weapon of choice wisely as you're limited by weight & class points. There are no cross hairs in this game so you have to decide if a laser sight is worth it or not or good old aiming down. Even if you play it offline the bots will tear you a new one, so brace yourself.

Love this game, nothin else like it! well worth the money. We need more guns though, the latest addition was nice but using the same gun variations over and over is getting old. its about time for a massive gun overhaul and update in my opinion!

It's fun and a great casual tactical shooter, but currently I am experiencing rubberbanding and other lag-esque issues despite low ping or high frames + low GPU usage, but that could be something on my end.

really good pve gamemodes that get really chaotic, pvp is also fine, the shooting at first is clunky but you get used to it, loads of weapons, bots will either be completely clueless or push and onetap you, the graphics are nothing crazy but they aren't bad, the game will run on 99% of computers
amazing and pure hearted community❤️❤️teamates willing to compliment you and be encouraging team players❤️❤️❤️

Multiplayer is full of tryhards, but we can blame that on skill issue on my part. I think its actually due to me being more used to realistic shooters like Arma or Squad, where you don't go running around straight into the enemy, but yeah this is an more arcade shooter, your life and impacts isn't important

PVE mode is surprisingly chill for how brutally realistic the game is supposed to be. The bots are just dangerous enough to keep things interesting, without being too dangerous to the point of feeling unfair all the time.

Its a good game and can be very fun when you play with friends but after some matches it just gets boring. Your playing on the same maps, the same gamemode and the same players since you dont pool with other players after the game ends.

i give it 9/10 the maps are well thought out, with plenty of variety, but what doesnt bring it a 10/10 is that the maps and guns dont seem "beautiful" rather just a very played out counterstrike terrorist theme, its nothing like counter strike, this is just the theme itself, it doesnt seem original in the theme, but its perfected all other aspects, i personally would have perferred like a world war theme, or a modern military theme, cowboys, or a unique theme. 

Gunplay is similar to rainbow six siege, or hunt, since theres such a high lethality you can some what reliably one tap someone accross the map with an AK, so its more about positioning, youll notice every player thats outsurvived you is usually holding angles, crouching, proning, scanning carefully, and moving slow, its a tactical shooter, so if you grab this game up dont run and gun like CoD, slice your corners and angles, you can also use Q and E to peak. look for dirty angles like shooting through a blown up car, or under it, dont stand right in door ways, back up a bit into the back drop of the room and shoot through the doorway.

As far as the maps go, they are similar sizes to battlefield maps but the pathing is so interesting and it doesnt just look like a generic map, theres so much that goes into these maps to make them feel like your not just walking through the same apartment building or office building in every single map, theres plenty of outside fights and inside, so sometimes youll be running through the mountains or forest.

The modes are also pretty solid, i dont normally like search and destroy but they have a unique take on it where theres a designated target who is armed with a silenced pistol, and you gotta escort him to one of two extraction points and extract him, if the enemy kills your "vip" target they win, if he extracts on A or B he wins, which is pretty unique, and they have plenty of other modes, one of my favorites are the attack and defend maps where you have to slowly push through the maps capturing objectives in order, bf has this one as well. its similar to hardcore modes, you dont even know if your single shot full killed someone until you push up and see their body

The people in the game definately have a microphone, almost all of them! every lobby feels like a different social circle. If you like hunt showdown / rainbow six seige or your just looking for a slower more tactical shooter feel, this is a great one to grab up
after many years of developing this game has many bad spawn locations in most of maps that enemies can camp and kill every one in one second

A good, honest, pack a punch, yet super fun shooter game. Vs AI is equally tough as they learn from players. Controls feel fluid and if you're not careful, you'll be punished. Speaking of which, you'll probably die alot when starting out but that's the challenge it offers to you. A fun shooter game if you're looking for quick, relaxing yet short burst round
Honestly quite a lot of fun. There are multiple interesting game modes that you dont really see out there much. The weapons feel good too.
good graphics, mechanics, optimization

If you enjoy tactical shooters with a splash of fun, this is it.  The Co-Op mode is exceptional, there is nothing like it in any other shooter I have played.  Also the new map is awesome!   I'll keep playing this till I can't anymore or the sequel shows up.
Great first person shooter, and simple. Will probably get up over 2,000 hours since the devs keep dropping fresh updates for this 'kinda old' game!

Very nice tactical shooter that requires you to talk and coordinate with your team, even when they are random. I mostly played pve now and even the A.i can jump scare you now and then and kill you in seconds.
i haven't played much PVP, but the PVE is so good in this game, its really fun

Insurgency Sandstorm is in its base form a fun game. It has both PvP and PvE game modes plus it gets updated at least once a quarter. 
Pros:
Modern Milsim that doesn't have a ton of features and gimmicks to learn
Matches aren't very long so you're not stuck in a slug fest for too long
Plenty of guns to choose from and ways to customize them for specific or broad scenarios
Decent amount of maps to pick from, plus night variants

Cons: 
Not too many people play this game anymore so sometimes getting matches takes time
Servers aren't prioritized for this game since Focus Entertainment purchased the dev studio and pushed the servers for Space Marine 2
If you have poor communication on your team you will suffer, plenty of toxic players on PvP, and some do exist on PvE as well.
Maps are basically giant corridors that funnel enemies into kill zones that can easily become one sided nightmares.

Overall i definitely recommend this game, it goes on sale often and for good discounts. If you have friends who play it even better. It can be repetitive but that has its charms for some players. 100% recommend Co op for the overall best experience since most of those guys are more welcoming to newer players.

Steep learning curve at first but fair and punishing realism. Also has the greatest game mode I've seen in a game where one side escorts a player who only has a pistol to extraction and the enemy only has to kill that one player to win the round.
highly realistic FPS, incredible immersive sounds amd cooperation agignst BOTS and online gameplay hihly recommend hope they will launch a new game to follow the spirit of sandstorm!

This a very captivating game. There was just a big download this week with 2 new maps. I hope that continues. The AI combat is great for beginners, and the P-v-P never gets old. Good bang for your $$$
Классная игра, что-то наподобие Battlefield с реалистичностью Arma 3, очень классная комбинация и есть чиловые режимы против ИИ

Insurgency: Sandstorm is one of the most intense and immersive tactical shooters I’ve played.

The sound design alone completely changes the experience. Gunshots crack through buildings, explosions feel overwhelming, and every fight feels dangerous because a single mistake can end you instantly.

What I appreciate most is that the game doesn’t try to be flashy or arcade-like. The slower pacing, teamwork, communication, and realism create tension that a lot of modern shooters are missing.

Every match feels chaotic in the most grounded way possible.
Very fun and realistic FPS shooter, would recommend 100%
its a strategic and fun game. You actually need comms with your team to win, and the community isn't too bad. People don't cuss you out and call you slurs (only happened twice to me). Fun game.

Having issues where the game tries to force me to link my epic games account whenever I start the game up. I only have 1 Epic Games account and no matter what I do it will not allow me to link the accounts together. Tried multiple different methods of trying to link the accounts, fix the errors and workarounds to not have to link the accounts at all but nothing works. Have owned this game since launch and have never had issues with it until now. Quite literally unplayable for me personally. I have no idea why they would force you to link an Epic Games account when you already bought the game, own the game, and launch the game through steam. Genuinely sick and tired of third party game launchers being mandatory to play steam games, through steam. At this current time, I cannot recommend this game.

I have played this game in little spurts over the many years since release and it seems like now it's in a good place with the new developer. I hope they keep up the updates though I do hope they do an engine upgrade at some point. It's definitely showing it's age.

Enjoyed playing. Eight individuals can plan together on a team. Fun game.

I like sandstorm but it could definitely have some improvements! I really like military simulations more but this is a pretty nice game where it's not a simulation but ballistics and everything is pretty nice. The thing is it's a little bit weird at first to get a hold of how everything works and how to actually play it and you need to figure out settings and definitely set up good bindings etc. 

Once you get into the game it's very fun the only real problem i have is there is no option to set the volume of the voices of in game characters constantly yelling they captured b, it would be fun to have servers where the sound effects of bullets and screaming and getting shot you can hear your character hurt but disable all that voices about capturing points so you can play with your teammates and speak through mics more.

That would make it more fun because you would actually communicate much more with your microphone and it would be a really good idea to implement such a feature. It gets kind of annoying after playing for hours constantly hearing your character talk they are taking b etc. 

Please implement a feature where you can disable or adjust the volume of these but keep the sound effects of character getting hurt etc. 

Otherwise, it would also be nice to implement bigger maps so you can actually explore it a bit more. 

Otherwise i like it, it's not a mil sim that you walk for 30 minutes without firing (even though i absolutely love actual mil sims a lot) but it's also not a typical shooter where you respawn every 2 seconds like counter strike which is unplayable. It's a pretty nice game! 

I would love Insurgency 3. It would really be nice and fresh if that ever comes out!!! I also played a lot of normal insurgency. And that game was awesome and in some things i actually prefer the first one but sandstorm is absolutely amazing you just need to get into it a bit. 

That's all i wanted to comment about the game, please make a sequel eventually!
realistic fun game to kill time fr small commiunity is great nd funny

Now look I showed up expecting a game that was sort of like Star Wars Battlefront 2 but COIN themed. (That means fighting insurgents)
So think sort of like Six Days in Falluja but not a milsim or anything and more accessible. That is not what I got, then again what did I expect from this genre, they're all like this.
Short-to-medium range only, the classes are locked behind grinding and don't seem that interesting. The maps are bland, untactical, kind of like CS2 but without the nice simplicity.
The guns feel small, kind of watery, and unpowerful in feel, even if they do a lot of DPS. I don't see snipers, suppression, or grenade launchers doing much here.
It's supposedly Counter-Insurgency (COIN) but it, like almost all other COIN games, is essentially a medium-range solo shooter with cosmetic grinding and the most un-COIN thing imaginable: Straight-forward, WW2 style tug-of-war territory stuff.

If you wanted to play as a US military equivalent (Or the US Military itself) and fight insurgent NPCs or something, this is not the right place, and I have been betrayed by this kind of marketing many, many times. This is a game where you do arbitary scavenging weapons off the ground, wander around shooting eachother, capture positions, and so on.

The in-depth firearms really aren't very interesting, or in-depth, it's mostly a gimmick that your gun technically doesn't shoot the exact center of the screen when not aiming down sights.

Well, the game lagged till the very first time. I've tried fixing it, looking for people who had same issues, but nothing helped. every second there is a freeze that lasts another second. 
Even if you get similar issues, and you successfully fix them, the fact that the game is broken and the developers can't fix it speaks for itself.

i se hyper realistic youtube videos and other stuff like that and when i get the game it looks NOTHING like that. Cheap ass game modes and i want to get to the action, not do survive the angry terrorists with Apache's and ♥♥♥♥

Just not very good, gun play feels somewhat weight less recoil while high does make sense. But your a newbie most have been playing this for years. I would say there are better shooters, more realistic shooters. It's a good way to waste some time, no way I would no life this. It's fun at times but can quickly get frustrating because of low player count meaning bad connection. I ran into several bugs such as my arms disappearing not being able to leave chat without sending a message and enemies just not being there when I was killed. I also got killed through wall by explosions a few times. I also have to mention mantling it is unintuitive situational and somewhat frustrating. I'll play some more but for a game that is eight years old I had wanted and expected more. The guns are amazing, kills do feel good to get but also leave me wanting.

also it's starting to crash a bit too often for some reason

I can only recommend this game with a gigantic asterisk, which is the community itself.

The game is great as a matter of software, having come from a group of very skilled people with all of the experience from the original Insurgency source mod.  It's fun, it's smooth, it runs well even on modern systems.  I can't think of a single thing that sticks out to me as truly "bad" about the game as the developers created it, besides maybe their parent company, Embracer Group, which you can read all about on Wikipedia.

The community, unfortunately, deserves no such praise.  As of mid-2026, you will still be able to queue for matches and find a game instantly pretty much any time of day, but if you play on US servers, fully expect some of the worst ankle-biting, racist, high-cortisol losers on the planet screaming slurs back and forth, no different from the COD lobbies of the early 2010s.  On off-days and weekends, you'll find more cool people on who are willing to chat and have fun and don't get angry when the numbers in the fake video game don't go up how they want them to.  In most lobbies, people just won't communicate though.  No-mic is about as common as the chuds.

There's also the classic problem that most long-running PVP games suffer from.  People who have been playing 5 or 6 [i]thousand[/i] hours, being matched with people who downloaded the game for the first time yesterday.  It's pretty bad for longevity and worse for enjoyment for anyone who isn't up to that level of skill.

The developers recently (within the last year or so) added crossplay, so everyone on PS and Xbox can help keep the thing up and running until the community drops off for good, and it does even out the skill gap for a lot of people even with modern gyro aiming, but whether the game continuing to go on as it is is a [i]good[/i] thing is anyone's call.

There are PVE modes built in, if you prefer to fight bots in co-op, and the level of sweat and bigotry here is significantly reduced, but still non-zero.

Insurgency: Sandstorm is a refreshing take on the tactical shooter genre, capturing the spirit of old-school, hardcore Call of Duty. The time-to-kill (TTK) is brutal—just one or two shots—and with no sliding or exaggerated movement, every encounter demands thought and precision. This lack of arcade gimmicks pushes you to act tactically—no mindless rushing, just careful planning around corners and positioning. I’ve played on a variety of hardware. From an old X99 setup with a Xeon E5-1650 v3 and 1080 just to see if it could do it. All the way up to my main rig with a 13900K and a 5070 Ti—and it runs smoothly across both spectrums with appropriate settings. . Both the PvP and PvE modes are strong; when you have a good team (and avoid toxic players), it’s an absolute blast. The weapons feel great, and the tactical depth really sets it apart. My only gripe is that enemy spawns in PvE can be a bit frustrating, but overall, I’ve had a fantastic time with it. If you love deliberate shooters with a high skill ceiling, this is a must-play
A fun mil sim had on console and once i got a pc it was one of the first games i got the slower style is not for everyone but very very fun i could sink 1000000 hours if possible
